captured_visuals.sync_01_source_before_edit.png: MemberAccount.ts in Claude Code editor pane — 7 fields (BEFORE)
captured_visuals.sync_02_source_after_edit.png: MemberAccount.ts after the developer adds email — 8 fields
captured_visuals.sync_03_robo_sync_running.png: /robo-sync mid-execution in the embedded Claude Code terminal — MCP tool calls visible
captured_visuals.sync_04_graph_after_api_response.png: Raw GET /api/contexts/{bc_id}/full-tree response showing MemberAccount.properties[] now contains 'email'
captured_visuals.sync_05_member_account_after_graph.json: Pretty-printed JSON slice of the MemberAccount node from the graph AFTER /robo-sync; email field present
captured_visuals.sync_06_aggregate_view_with_email.png: SPA Aggregate tab — navigator shows MembershipManagement (core) expanded with MemberAccount visible

ui_limitation_noted: The Aggregate Viewer's canvas requires HTML5 drag-and-drop from the navigator. Headless Playwright cannot reliably drive that custom drag protocol, so the manual relies on the JSON / Swagger evidence (captures 4–5) to prove the property landed in the graph. Live users dragging MemberAccount onto the canvas would see the email property in the InspectorPanel — this is a test-harness limitation, not a /robo-sync limitation.
